title: coryphantha macromeris subsp. runyonii
intro: Coryphantha macromeris subsp. runyonii, commonly known as the Runyon's pincushion cactus, is a type of cactus that belongs to the Echinocereus genus. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation due to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids. The Runyon's pincushion cactus is said to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which may help to prevent cell damage, reduce pain and swelling, and fight off infections. It can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, although its use should be carefully considered and consulted with a medical professional due to potential interactions with medications and allergies.
